questions.question: What are the two branches of the autonomic nervous system?

options.text: Sympathetic (fight or flight) and parasympathetic (rest and digest)

questions.explanation: Your autonomic nervous system has two branches: the sympathetic system (fight or flight, the 'gas pedal') and the parasympathetic system (rest and digest, the 'brake pedal'). Sleep is essential for engaging the repair mode.

questions.question: By how much can one night of poor sleep reduce T-cell function?
questions.explanation: Research shows that even one night of poor sleep can reduce T-cell function by up to 70%, significantly weakening your immune defense against infections.

questions.question: What are cytokines and what role do they play during sleep?

options.text: Signaling proteins that coordinate immune response, produced in greater quantities during sleep

questions.explanation: Cytokines are signaling proteins that coordinate your immune response. They are produced in greater quantities during sleep and help direct immune cells to sites of infection and inflammation.

questions.question: Why is sleep described as the most powerful tool for engaging your body's repair mode?

options.text: Because it increases your heart rate
Because it is the primary time your body spends in parasympathetic mode, enabling deep repair, restoration, and immune strengthening
Because it activates the sympathetic nervous system

questions.explanation: Sleep is your body's primary time in parasympathetic mode. This is when deep repair, restoration, and immune strengthening happen, including tissue repair, hormone release, and immune cell production.

questions.question: Why might getting good rest before and after vaccinations be beneficial?

options.text: Antibody production increases during sleep, improving vaccine effectiveness
It speeds up the injection site healing

questions.explanation: Antibody production increases during sleep, which is why getting good rest before and after vaccinations can improve their effectiveness. Sleep supports your immune system's ability to respond to the vaccine.
